page: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-3200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, running on an x86_64 AMD EPYC processor. The CPU architecture consists of 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz under a shared allocation model. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, supporting instruction sets such as AVX, AES, SHA_NI, and BMI2.

This server is equipped with 3200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, providing substantial capacity for data-intensive applications. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests and a top-tier PassMark memory score. Memory bandwidth is strong for large-block read and write operations, while multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and static web serving throughput align with mid-tier averages.

Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core and large local NVMe storage, this configuration offers a cost-effective profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. The shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention tradeoffs, but the generous memory and storage density make it highly suitable for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-bound data processing tasks.
description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-3200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance configured with 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM on an x86_64 AMD EPYC processor. Operating with 8 physical cores and a shared CPU allocation at 2.0 GHz, this server includes 3200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside strong large-block memory bandwidth. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core and substantial NVMe capacity provide qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense workloads, though the shared CPU model presents a tradeoff for sustained multi-core processing. This server is well-suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive data processing.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-3200s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 16 shared vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3200 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-3200s-amd is an AMD EPYC instance with 16 shared vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 3200 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity memory and NVMe storage instance utilizing shared AMD EPYC processors for data-intensive and memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB RAM on a shared AMD EPYC processor architecture
3200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-capacity local data requirements
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and PassMark memory benchmark scores
Strong memory bandwidth for large-block read and write operations
Suited for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-bound processing
